summary: The review of the Data Transfer Agreement reveals a moderate to high-risk posture due to critical deficiencies in confidentiality and intellectual property clauses. The confidentiality provisions lack essential exclusions, duration specifications, and alignment with UoA Position #POS-11, while multiple intellectual property clauses require referral to Auckland UniServices Ltd for compliance with UoA Position #POS-IP. Immediate escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, and Auckland UniServices Ltd is recommended to address these flagged issues and ensure the agreement aligns with institutional policies.

flags.clause_title: Confidentiality and Data Sharing Agreement
flags.snippet: Confidentiality and Data Sharing Agreement
flags.rationale: The clause text provided is too vague to confirm alignment with UoA Position #POS-11 on Confidentiality. It does not specify exclusions for public information, independently developed information, or information legally required to be disclosed. Additionally, it does not address the ability for UoA to disclose to agents and Affiliates or the duration of confidentiality obligations. Without these critical details, the clause falls outside the Acceptable tier and requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review.

flags.clause_title: C. The Recipient has agreed to keep confidential the information disclo
flags.snippet: C. The Recipient has agreed to keep confidential the information disclosed to it by the Discloser.
flags.rationale: The clause does not align with UoA Position #POS-11 on Confidentiality. It lacks critical elements such as exclusions for information already public, independently developed, previously known, or legally required to be disclosed. Additionally, it does not specify a duration for confidentiality obligations, which is required to meet either the Preferred or Acceptable tier. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is necessary to address these deficiencies.

flags.clause_title: 1.1 Background Intellectual Property means Intellectual Property prepar
flags.snippet: 1.1 Background Intellectual Property means Intellectual Property prepared or developed prior to the Execution Date by any party, and includes improvements to such Intellectual Property developed during the period of thi…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses Intellectual Property, specifically Background IP and improvements to it. UoA Contracting Positions explicitly state that all IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review, as UoA does not set IP positions independently. This clause falls under the 'acceptable' tier for referral.

flags.clause_title: 1.12 Intellectual Property means all intellectual and industrial proper
flags.snippet: 1.12 Intellectual Property means all intellectual and industrial property rights and includes any patent, design, copyright, trade mark, plant breeders rights, technology, know-how, and Confidential Information, and all...
flags.rationale: The clause defines Intellectual Property, which falls under the topic of IP. UoA Contracting Positions explicitly state that all IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review, regardless of wording. This clause is therefore flagged as AMBER for referral.

flags.clause_title: (a) de-identified clinical data and other information, records, contain
flags.snippet: (a) de-identified clinical data and other information, records, containing Intellectual Property;
flags.rationale: The clause involves Intellectual Property, which is explicitly excluded from UoA Contracting Positions and must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review. This aligns with UoA Position #POS-IP, which mandates referral of all IP-related clauses regardless of wording.

flags.clause_title: (c) all unpatented inventions (including those the subject of a patent
flags.snippet: (c) all unpatented inventions (including those the subject of a patent application), ideas, know-how, concepts, trade secrets, processes, techniques, software, products and all other intellectual property, financial and business information and all other commercially valuable information relating to the Discloser which the Discloser regards as confidential, and includes any such information in the Discloser’s possession, power or control concerning or belonging to any other person;
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, which is explicitly outside the scope of UoA Contracting Positions and must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review. This aligns with UoA Position #POS-IP, which mandates referral of all IP clauses regardless of wording.

flags.clause_title: other intellectual property as defined in Article 2 of the convention o
flags.snippet: other intellectual property as defined in Article 2 of the convention of 1967 establishing the World Intellectual Property Organisation;
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, which is explicitly outside the scope of UoA's Preferred Contracting Positions. All IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices (Head of IP) for review, as per UoA Position #POS-IP. This clause is flagged as AMBER because it requires further review by UniServices.

Without further context or definition, it cannot be assessed against the Preferred or Acceptable tiers. This clause requires further review to determine compliance.

flags.clause_title: (b) Privacy Act;
flags.snippet: (b) Privacy Act; 1.17 Purpose means solely for clinical research to be conducted by the Recipient and for no other purpose; 1.18 Principles means, as the context so permits the principles governing the collection, use, management, disclosure, transfer and destruction of: (a) Health Information in Victoria, Australia and are made pursuant to the Health Records Act; (b) data and Personal Information in Victoria, Australia made pursuant to the Privacy and Data Protection Act; and (c) Personal Information in New Zealand made pursuant to the Privacy Act;
flags.rationale: The clause primarily addresses privacy and data protection laws, including the Privacy Act 2020 (NZ) and equivalent laws in Victoria, Australia. This topic is not covered by any specific UoA Preferred Contracting Position or template. While it touches on confidentiality and data protection, it does not directly address liability limitations or exclusions as flagged by the comparator.
